_note: All settings in this file work on a standard self-hosted Elasticsearch cluster with a free Basic license and no plugins required. Removed: soft_deletes.retention_lease.period (requires CCR / Platinum license), routing_partition_size (requires enforced _routing in mappings), shard.check_on_startup (expert diagnostic use only).

index._comment_number_of_shards: [STATIC] Number of primary shards. Cannot be changed after index creation. Max 1024.
index._comment_number_of_routing_shards: [STATIC] Controls how many shards the index can be split into in the future via the Split API. Defaults internally based on number_of_shards. Only set if you plan to use the Split API later.
index._comment_codec: [STATIC] Compression for stored fields. 'default' = LZ4 (fast). 'best_compression' = ZSTD (~28% smaller, slower reads).
index._comment_soft_deletes.enabled: [STATIC] Enables soft deletes so deleted/updated documents are retained temporarily as history. Required for Sequence Number tracking. Default true since ES 7.0, do not disable.
index._comment_load_fixed_bitset_filters_eager: [STATIC] Pre-loads cached bitset filters for nested queries when a shard opens. Improves nested query performance at the cost of slightly slower shard startup.
index._comment_number_of_replicas: [DYNAMIC] Number of replica shards per primary. Set to 0 during bulk indexing to speed up ingestion, restore afterward. More replicas = better read throughput and fault tolerance.
index._comment_auto_expand_replicas: [DYNAMIC] Automatically scale replicas based on cluster node count. Format: '0-5' or '0-all'. false = disabled. Useful for single-node dev clusters.
index._comment_refresh_interval: [DYNAMIC] How often the index is refreshed to make newly indexed documents visible to search. '-1' disables refresh entirely (useful during bulk indexing). Default '1s'.
index._comment_max_result_window: [DYNAMIC] Maximum value of 'from + size' for search and get requests. Raising this is memory-expensive. Prefer search_after or scroll for deep pagination.
index._comment_max_inner_result_window: [DYNAMIC] Maximum 'from + size' for inner hits (nested / parent-child queries) and top_hits aggregations.
index._comment_max_rescore_window: [DYNAMIC] Maximum window_size for rescore requests. Carries the same memory cost concern as max_result_window.
index._comment_max_docvalue_fields_search: [DYNAMIC] Maximum number of docvalue_fields that can be requested per search query.
index._comment_max_script_fields: [DYNAMIC] Maximum number of script_fields allowed per search request.
index._comment_max_ngram_diff: [DYNAMIC] Maximum allowed difference between min_gram and max_gram when using the NGram tokenizer. Raise if you need wider ngram ranges.
index._comment_max_shingle_diff: [DYNAMIC] Maximum allowed difference between min_shingle_size and max_shingle_size for the shingle token filter.
index._comment_max_terms_count: [DYNAMIC] Maximum number of terms that can be passed in a single Terms query.
index._comment_max_regex_length: [DYNAMIC] Maximum character length of a regex pattern used in Regexp or Wildcard queries.
index._comment_gc_deletes: [DYNAMIC] How long a deleted document's version number is retained for optimistic concurrency control. After this window, the version is gone and cannot be referenced.
index._comment_default_pipeline: [DYNAMIC] Name of the ingest pipeline to run on every index/bulk request by default. null = no pipeline. Overridable per request via ?pipeline=name.
index._comment_final_pipeline: [DYNAMIC] Name of the ingest pipeline that always runs last, after any default or request-level pipeline. null = no pipeline.
index._comment_hidden: [DYNAMIC] When true, hides the index from wildcard expressions (e.g. GET /*). The index must be addressed by its exact name.
index._comment_priority: [DYNAMIC] Recovery priority after a cluster restart. Higher integer = this index is recovered before lower-priority indices.
index._comment_routing.allocation.enable: [DYNAMIC] Controls which shard types can be allocated for this index. 'all' = all shards, 'primaries' = primary only, 'new_primaries' = new primaries only, 'none' = no allocation.
index._comment_routing.allocation.total_shards: [DYNAMIC] Maximum number of shards (primary + replica) of this index that can be placed on a single node. -1 = unlimited.
index._comment_routing.allocation.include: [DYNAMIC] Only allocate shards to nodes that have AT LEAST ONE of the specified node attribute values. e.g. { 'zone': 'zone1' }.
index._comment_routing.allocation.exclude: [DYNAMIC] Never allocate shards to nodes that have any of the specified node attribute values.
index._comment_routing.allocation.require: [DYNAMIC] Only allocate shards to nodes that have ALL of the specified node attribute values.
index._comment_routing.rebalance.enable: [DYNAMIC] Controls shard rebalancing for this index. 'all', 'primaries', 'replicas', or 'none'.
index._comment_merge.policy.max_merge_at_once: [DYNAMIC] Maximum number of segments merged together in a single background merge operation.
index._comment_merge.policy.segments_per_tier: [DYNAMIC] Number of segments per tier in the tiered merge policy. Lower = fewer total segments but more frequent merges.
index._comment_merge.policy.expunge_deletes_al: [DYNAMIC] Percentage of deleted documents a segment may contain before it becomes eligible for an expunge-deletes merge.
index._comment_merge.policy.floor_segment: [DYNAMIC] Minimum segment size below which a segment is always eligible to be merged. Prevents accumulation of many tiny segments.
index._comment_merge.policy.max_merged_segment: [DYNAMIC] Maximum size a merged segment can reach. Segments above this size will never be selected for merging.
index._comment_merge.policy.reclaim_deletes_we: [DYNAMIC] Weighting given to reclaiming deleted document space during merges. Higher value = more aggressive reclamation of deletes.
index._comment_indexing.slowlog.threshold.inde: [DYNAMIC] Emit a WARN slow log entry for indexing operations that exceed this duration. '-1' disables. Example: '10s'.
[DYNAMIC] Emit an INFO slow log entry for indexing operations that exceed this duration. '-1' disables.
[DYNAMIC] Emit a DEBUG slow log entry for indexing operations that exceed this duration. '-1' disables.
[DYNAMIC] Emit a TRACE slow log entry for indexing operations that exceed this duration. '-1' disables.
index._comment_indexing.slowlog.source: [DYNAMIC] Maximum number of _source characters to include in slow log entries. 0 = none, -1 = full source.
[DYNAMIC] Emit a WARN slow log entry for search query phase operations exceeding this duration. '-1' disables. Example: '5s'.
[DYNAMIC] Emit an INFO slow log entry for search query phase operations exceeding this duration. '-1' disables.
[DYNAMIC] Emit a DEBUG slow log entry for search query phase operations exceeding this duration. '-1' disables.
[DYNAMIC] Emit a TRACE slow log entry for search query phase operations exceeding this duration. '-1' disables.
[DYNAMIC] Emit a WARN slow log entry for search fetch phase operations exceeding this duration.
[DYNAMIC] Emit an INFO slow log entry for search fetch phase operations exceeding this duration.
[DYNAMIC] Emit a DEBUG slow log entry for search fetch phase operations exceeding this duration.
[DYNAMIC] Emit a TRACE slow log entry for search fetch phase operations exceeding this duration.
index._comment_translog.durability: [DYNAMIC] 'request' = fsync after every operation (safe, default). 'async' = fsync on interval (faster writes, small risk of data loss on hard crash).
index._comment_translog.flush_threshold_size: [DYNAMIC] Flush the translog and create a new Lucene commit point when the translog reaches this size.
index._comment_translog.sync_interval: [DYNAMIC] How often the translog is fsynced to disk when durability is set to 'async'. Minimum value is 100ms.
index._comment_store.type: [STATIC] Filesystem implementation used for shard storage. 'hybridfs' (default) = best choice per file type automatically. Other options: 'niofs', 'mmapfs', 'fs'.
index._comment_store.preload: [STATIC] List of file extensions to eagerly load into the OS filesystem cache when a shard opens. e.g. ['nvd', 'dvd']. Use carefully on large indices as it consumes RAM.
index._comment_sort.field: [STATIC] Field or list of fields to sort documents by at index time. Speeds up queries and aggregations that sort/range-filter on this field. null = no index-time sorting.
index._comment_sort.order: [STATIC] Sort direction for index-time sorting. 'asc' or 'desc'. Only relevant when sort.field is set.
index._comment_sort.mode: [STATIC] How to pick a representative value for multi-value fields during index-time sorting. 'min' or 'max'. null = not applicable for single-value fields.
index._comment_sort.missing: [STATIC] Where to place documents that are missing the sort field. '_last' = end of results, '_first' = beginning.
index._comment_source.mode: [STATIC] Controls how _source is stored. 'stored' = full source written to disk (default). 'synthetic' = reconstructed on the fly at read time (saves disk space). 'disabled' = no _source stored at all.
index._comment_blocks.read_only: [DYNAMIC] Makes the index fully read-only — no writes, deletes, or metadata changes. Often set automatically by Elasticsearch when a disk watermark is breached.
index._comment_blocks.read_only_allow_delete: [DYNAMIC] Read-only mode but document deletion is still permitted. Set automatically by Elasticsearch when disk is critically full.
index._comment_blocks.read: [DYNAMIC] Disables all read operations (searches and gets) on the index.
index._comment_blocks.write: [DYNAMIC] Disables all write operations (index, update, delete) while still allowing metadata changes.
index._comment_blocks.metadata: [DYNAMIC] Disables all metadata changes on the index, including settings updates, alias changes, and mapping updates.
index._comment_mapping.total_fields.limit: [DYNAMIC] Maximum total number of fields (including object, nested, and metadata fields) in the index mapping. Guards against mapping explosion from dynamic field creation.
index._comment_mapping.depth.limit: [DYNAMIC] Maximum nesting depth for object fields. Each level of object/nested nesting counts toward this limit.
index._comment_mapping.nested_fields.limit: [DYNAMIC] Maximum number of distinct nested field type definitions across the entire index mapping.
index._comment_mapping.nested_objects.limit: [DYNAMIC] Maximum number of nested JSON objects a single document can contain in total across all nested fields.
index._comment_mapping.field_name_length.limit: [DYNAMIC] Maximum character length allowed for field names. null = no enforced limit.
index._comment_similarity: [STATIC] Custom named similarity (relevance scoring) configurations. Each entry is assignable to fields in mappings by name. e.g. { 'my_bm25': { 'type': 'BM25', 'k1': 1.5, 'b': 0.75 } }. Built-in types: BM25, boolean, DFR, IB, LMDirichlet, LMJelinekMercer.

analysis._comment: [STATIC] All analysis settings require the index to be closed before they can be updated on an existing index.
analysis._comment_analyzer: Named custom analyzers. Each combines one tokenizer with optional char_filters and token filters. e.g. { 'my_analyzer': { 'type': 'custom', 'tokenizer': 'standard', 'filter': ['lowercase', 'asciifolding'] } }.
analysis._comment_filter: Custom token filter definitions applied after tokenization. e.g. { 'my_stop': { 'type': 'stop', 'stopwords': ['the', 'a', 'is'] } }.
analysis._comment_char_filter: Custom character filter definitions applied before tokenization. e.g. { 'html_strip': { 'type': 'html_strip' } } or mapping char filters.
analysis._comment_normalizer: Named normalizers for keyword fields (no tokenizer — filters only). e.g. { 'lowercase_norm': { 'type': 'custom', 'filter': ['lowercase', 'asciifolding'] } }.
